stories.headline: Jerome Powell to remain on Fed board until DOJ probe concludes
Fed holds rates steady; Powell signals economy remains resilient above 2% growth threshold
stories.summary: Federal Reserve concluded Powell's final meeting by holding rates steady while signaling continued economic strength and dismissing recession concerns, though internal divisions emerged on the path forward.
stories.insight: Powell's decision to stay through the DOJ investigation removes lame-duck uncertainty at a critical juncture when markets are pricing in sticky inflation and geopolitical oil shocks; this eliminates succession risk during a period when Fed credibility on inflation forecasting is being tested by higher energy costs.

stories.headline: Meta raises 2026 AI capex to $125B-$145B; stock sinks on efficiency concerns
stories.summary: Meta disclosed a massive upward revision of artificial intelligence spending for 2026, signaling aggressive infrastructure bets on large language models and data centers, triggering market sell-off.
stories.insight: Meta's capex guidance increase reveals AI spending is now decoupled from near-term revenue impact; this creates a structural test of whether scale advantages and network effects justify $60B+ incremental annual investment or whether the market views this as overcapitalization chasing uncertain AI monetization paths.

stories.headline: Oil briefly hits $120 amid Iran blockade reports; US fuel prices climb despite low OPEC import dependence
stories.summary: Crude spiked toward $120 on geopolitical risk from Iran tensions, pushing U.S. gasoline prices higher even though America imports minimal OPEC oil, revealing hidden supply chain and refining exposure.
stories.insight: Oil markets are pricing in global refining bottlenecks and shipping route disruptions that bypass direct OPEC import flows; this creates margin expansion for integrated refiners and hedging demand for crude regardless of domestic sourcing, making energy inflation a geopolitical transmission mechanism independent of energy independence narratives.

stories.headline: House advances 3-year extension of federal surveillance program
stories.summary: Congress moved forward on renewing expanded surveillance authorities for an additional three years, continuing broad data collection powers despite privacy advocacy concerns.
stories.insight: Surveillance reauthorization reflects persistent institutional bias toward counterterrorism and national security apparatus expansion; each three-year renewal converts temporary emergency powers into permanent infrastructure, reducing political friction for indefinite continuance and shifting the burden to fund privacy-protective alternatives.

stories.headline: Big tech earnings week begins: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft report today as $16T valuation faces investor scrutiny
stories.summary: Technology megacaps entered earnings season amid elevated AI capex expectations and questions about whether revenue growth justifies infrastructure spending acceleration.
stories.insight: Tech earnings window is now a referendum on AI monetization viability; if guidance suggests revenue acceleration below capex growth rates, capital allocation asymmetries widen and sector multiples compress, forcing redeployment toward profitable legacy businesses and spurring M&A consolidation around proven AI applications.

stories.headline: Oil prices spike to $120 on Iran blockade fears; geopolitical risk reshaping energy markets
stories.summary: Crude briefly crossed $120 per barrel as reports emerged of extended Iranian blockade measures, amplifying supply shock concerns across global petroleum markets.
stories.insight: Iran escalation is now the marginal price setter for crude; markets are pricing in logistics disruptions, insurance premium escalation, and potential forced rerouting through longer routes, creating a structural energy cost floor independent of traditional OPEC production decisions and generating inflation that constrains central bank policy flexibility.

stories.headline: Universal Music sells half Spotify stake for buybacks; weak dollar pressures Q1 earnings
stories.summary: Universal Music Group announced a partial divestiture of its Spotify equity position to fund shareholder repurchases, citing currency headwinds that dampened first-quarter revenue.
stories.insight: Music industry capital returns signal confidence in streaming cash flows but also reflect limited organic growth and FX drag reducing reported earnings; this forces investors to distinguish between dollar-denominated shareholder value creation versus headline growth metrics, creating arbitrage between domestic and global music company valuations.

stories.headline: Middlesex DA flags 142 cases linked to State Police detective facing homicide charge
stories.summary: Prosecutors identified 142 cases potentially compromised by a Massachusetts State Police detective who now faces homicide charges, triggering case review protocols and creating significant criminal justice system liability.
stories.insight: Large-scale detective misconduct creates systemic validation problems for prosecution records across decades of cases; this imposes discovery obligations, appeal pressures, and institutional costs that exceed direct case impact, forcing asset reallocation toward case review and potentially weakening conviction rate metrics used in prosecutorial performance assessment.

the_edge: Tech earnings are colliding with energy shock economics: Meta's $60B capex increase compounds cost pressures from Iran-driven oil spikes, while Fed rate hold removes policy relief. This creates a margin squeeze for data centers and downstream cloud consumers, forcing capital allocation choices between AI infrastructure and traditional earnings buybacks. The winners will be companies with captive energy (nuclear, renewable), the losers those dependent on grid pricing during supply constraints.
tomorrow_watch: Apple earnings after market close (capex intensity test); PCE inflation print Friday (gauging Iran oil impact on Fed decision trajectory); any escalation signals from Iran regarding broader blockade extension.
